scope_summary: State Act for the prevention, control and extinguishment of bush fires across Western Australia. It empowers the Minister and FES Commissioner to declare prohibited and restricted burning times and total fire bans, regulates burning permits and garden refuse burning, lets local governments require occupiers to plough or clear fire-breaks (s 33), and provides for bush fire risk treatment standards (Part III Division 7) and bush fire brigades. It applies to land statewide, including residential lots in bushfire-prone districts subject to local government fire-break notices.

key_numeric_standards.topic: fire-break width before burning land adjoining exempt land
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: occupier of land adjoining exempt land burning during prohibited times

key_numeric_standards.topic: maximum distance of fire-break from exempt land boundary
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: fire-break parallel to common boundary with exempt land

key_numeric_standards.value: 20 m apart maximum; each at least 2 m wide
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: burning during prohibited burning times under permit
burning subterranean clover during prohibited burning times
